title: paspalum affine
intro: Paspalum affine, also known as Bahamagrass, is a type of warm-season grass native to the Americas. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its saponin content. The health benefits of Paspalum affine include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antifungal agent. In herbal preparations, Paspalum affine is often made into a tea or infusion, and its essential oil is used topically to treat wounds and skin irritations.
